_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize GA batch: 5 central/north-GA legislators (barrett HD24-R, anderson SD24-R, williams SD25-R, todd-jones HD25-R w/ SB233 voucher deviation, david-lucas SD26-D straight-line). State tier. Evidence-first; null where no cited evidence; every TRUE/FALSE cited; party-line marquee votes credited per runbook. Lucas: Freedom Index + Grokipedia falsely listed conservative votes that official roll calls disprove — scored only verified contrary votes.

profile.religion: Non-denominational Christian (North Point Community Church, Alpharetta)
profile.background: State representative HD-24 (south Forsyth Co./Cumming, north Atlanta metro) since Jan 2023; reelected Nov 2024. UGA BBA finance; career banker (co-founded Community Business Bank; Affinity Bank N. Atlanta market president; Dime Capital). Committees: Higher Education (Vice Chair), Banks & Banking (Sec.), Ways & Means, Appropriations, Code Revision (Sec.). Confirmed running 2026 — no candidacy change found.

carter-barrett@GA.notes_set: Solid conservative; scored from a HARD SB 140 vote, authored anti-CBDC legislation, a police endorsement, and clear campaign-stated positions. Authored HB 1053 (prohibits GA agencies accepting CBDC payments). Voted YES on SB 140 (gender-transition ban for minors), House 96-75, 3/16/2023. Backed HB 974/HB 1207/SB 189 (paper ballots, watermarks, risk-limiting audits) and HB 1105 (anti-sanctuary immigration). Endorsed by Police Benevolent Assn of GA; backed HB 19 (LEO pay). Faith & Freedom A+, AFP 100, FreedomIndex 72% (composite ratings — not mapped to individual cells). Not listed on GLA 2024 cert (absence, not contrary). No conservative deviations. Individual SB 233/SB 63/SB 222 roll calls not retrievable → null, not inferred.

0.note: Campaign: 'will fight hard in the Georgia House to protect life from conception' (SOFT campaign self-statement).

2.note: Voted YES on SB 140 (ban on gender-transition hormones/surgery for minors), House passage 96-75, 3/16/2023 (HARD).

0.note: Campaign Issues page: 'I will prioritize school choice'; one-size-fits-all model 'does not work' (SOFT campaign self-statement).

2.note: Campaign: will work with school boards to 'keep CRT out of schools' (SOFT campaign self-statement).

0.note: Authored HB 1053 to prohibit GA governmental agencies from accepting payments in a central bank digital currency; calls a CBDC federal 'control over our lives' (HARD authored legislation).
Supported HB 974, HB 1207, SB 189 addressing paper ballots, watermarks on paper ballots, and expanded risk-limiting audits (self-reported bill support).
Endorsed by the Police Benevolent Association of Georgia; backed HB 19 (law-enforcement pay raises) — affirmative pro-law-enforcement (HARD endorsement).

2.note: Backed HB 1105 (GA Criminal Alien Track and Report Act — bans sanctuary policies, immigration-status checks, honors ICE detainers) (self-reported bill support).

profile.background: State senator SD-24 (Columbia, Elbert, Greene, Hart, Lincoln, Oglethorpe, Wilkes cos., Augusta metro) since Jan 2017. Farmer on family land farmed since the early 1900s; ABAC. Prior: GA House HD-117 (2009-12); GOP US House nominee GA-12 (2012, lost to Barrow). Chairman, Natural Resources & Environment; Vice Chair, Agriculture & Consumer Affairs; Appropriations; Rules. Confirmed running 2026 GOP primary (May 19) — no candidacy change.

lee-anderson@GA.notes_set: Consistent conservative with a documented sponsorship/voting record. Sponsor: SB 202 (Election Integrity Act, photo-ID absentee), SB 319 (constitutional carry), SB 420 (foreign-national farmland restrictions); co-sponsor SB 222 (Zuckerbucks ban, also YES on all 4 recorded Senate votes — note Max Burns was lead sponsor), SB 92 (rogue-prosecutor oversight commission); co-sponsor SB 390 (anti-ALA library bill). HARD votes: SB 233 (school choice) YES; SB 63 (cash-bail) YES on passage and final. NRA member + A-rating endorsement (2016); GRTL endorsement (2012 US House race). SB 140 and HB 1105 credited as party-line Senate marquee votes (sitting R on recorded side). No cross-party deviations found. Christian-liberty and federalism: no specific record → null (not inferred).

0.note: Georgia Right to Life endorsement (2012 US House race) — documented pro-life endorsement (HARD, though dated).

2.note: SB 140 (gender-transition ban for minors) passed Senate 31-21 party-line, 3/2023; sitting R on the recorded conservative side (party-line marquee vote).

0.note: Voted YES on SB 233 (Georgia Promise Scholarship / school-choice vouchers), Senate passage 33-23, 3/6/2023 (HARD).

1.note: Sponsor/co-sponsor of SB 202 (Election Integrity Act of 2021), which tightened absentee photo-ID/verification requirements (HARD sponsorship).

4.note: Co-sponsor of SB 222 (private-election-funding 'Zuckerbucks' ban) + YES on all four recorded Senate votes, 2023 (HARD).

1.note: Voted YES on SB 63 (cash-bail expansion; restricts charitable bail funds), passage #75 2/23/2023 and final conference report 2/1/2024; co-sponsored SB 92 (rogue-prosecutor oversight) — opposes cashless-bail/rogue-prosecutor (HARD).

2.note: HB 1105 (anti-sanctuary; penalizes local govts with loose immigration policies) passed Senate 34-19, 3/21/2024; sitting R on recorded conservative side (party-line marquee vote).

4.note: Sponsor of SB 420 (restricts certain foreign nationals/adversary nations from acquiring GA farmland, esp. near military bases) (HARD sponsorship).

0.note: Sponsor/co-sponsor of SB 319 (Georgia Constitutional Carry Act of 2021, permitless carry), enacted 2022 (HARD sponsorship).

1.note: NRA member + NRA A-rating endorsement (2016 Senate race) — opposes red-flag/registry/AWB-style gun control (HARD endorsement).

profile.religion: United Methodist (First United Methodist Church, Milledgeville)
profile.background: State senator SD-25 (Baldwin/Milledgeville + Butts, Jasper, Jones, Putnam, parts of Bibb/Henry, central GA) since Jan 2023; reelected Nov 2024. Funeral-home owner (Williams Funeral Homes, Milledgeville). Former Baldwin Co. Commissioner and Chief Registrar. Prior: GA House HD-145 (2017-22). Chairman, Retirement; Secretary, Public Safety; Secretary, Regulated Industries & Utilities; Vice Chair, Ethics. Incumbent through Jan 2027; 2026 ballot qualification not yet confirmed in accessible sources but no candidacy change found.

rick-williams@GA.notes_set: Documented conservative with confirmed recorded votes. HARD votes: SB 140 (gender-transition ban for minors, co-sponsor + YES 3/6/2023); SB 233 (school-choice vouchers) YES; SB 189 (election integrity — restricts QR-code tabulation, text-portion counting) YES 2/6/2024; SB 63 (cash-bail) YES 2/23/2023; HB 1105 (anti-sanctuary immigration) YES 3/21/2024. Primary sponsor SB 390 (anti-ALA library bill). Georgia Life Alliance 'Advocate for Life' award (2021). SOFT campaign: back-the-blue, fully fund police, pro-2A. SB 319 (constitutional carry) credited as party-line House marquee (he was a House R in 2022). No cross-party deviations. Tax/CBDC/federalism specifics not documented → null.

0.note: Georgia Life Alliance 'Advocate For Life' award (2021); campaign self-identifies pro-life and credits carrying the Heartbeat Bill (issue-group recognition).

2.note: Co-sponsor of and YES vote on SB 140 (gender-transition ban for minors), Senate final passage 33-22, 3/6/2023 (HARD).

0.note: Voted YES on SB 233 (Georgia Promise Scholarship / school-choice vouchers), Senate passage 33-23, 3/6/2023 (HARD).
Voted YES on SB 189 (2024 election-integrity bill — restricts QR-code ballot tabulation in favor of text-portion counting), Senate passage 31-22, 2/6/2024 (HARD).
Campaign: 'fully funding our police, and backing the blue'; serves as Secretary of the Senate Public Safety Committee (SOFT affirmative pro-law-enforcement).

1.note: Voted YES on SB 63 (cash-bail expansion; limits charitable bail funds), Senate passage 31-23, 2/23/2023 — opposes cashless-bail (HARD).

2.note: Voted YES on HB 1105 (Criminal Alien Track and Report Act / anti-sanctuary), Senate passage 34-19, 3/21/2024 (HARD).

0.note: SB 319 (constitutional/permitless carry) passed House 100-67 party-line 3/30/2022 while he was a House R; campaign affirms defending the right to bear arms (party-line marquee vote).

profile.religion: Not publicly documented (no church affiliation located)
profile.background: State representative HD-25 (south Forsyth + north Fulton cos., north Atlanta metro) since Jan 2017. JD & BBA-finance, University of Florida; business executive (Cambridge Technology Partners, Accenture). Chairman, Technology & Infrastructure Innovation Committee; also Appropriations, Education, Health, Judiciary, Energy/Utilities & Telecom. WON the May 19, 2026 GOP primary 87.5% (def. Mathew Philip) — advances to November general.

todd-jones@GA.notes_set: Mostly conservative voting record (FreedomIndex 59% cumulative / 55% current session) with ONE notable cross-party deviation: voted NO on SB 233 (Georgia Promise Scholarship vouchers) — against the flagship school-choice bill, contradicting his own campaign 'increase choice in education' claim. HARD YES votes: SB 140 (gender-transition ban for minors), HB 1084 (trans-sports + CRT restrictions), HB 1178 (Parents' Bill of Rights), SB 36 (RFRA), HB 1053 (CBDC prohibition), opposed HB 221 state-debt increase, HR 780 (citizen-only voting amendment), SB 1/SB 345 (resist federal COVID/vaccine mandates), SB 420 (foreign-owned farmland), SB 319 (constitutional carry). Excused / NO recorded YES on HB 481 (2019 heartbeat) and voted NO on HB 428 (right-to-IVF, ambiguous) → no pro-life cell scored (honest null, not a FALSE). SOFT campaign back-the-blue → public_justice 0. Religion genuinely undocumented; prior 'God First' note belonged to opponent Mathew Philip (a pastor), corrected. SB 222/SB 63/SB 92/HB 1105 individual roll calls not retrievable → null. NRA/GRTL ratings not located.

2.note: Voted YES on SB 140 (gender-transition ban for minors) and HB 1084 (transgender-sports ban + CRT restrictions) (HARD).

4.note: Voted YES on HB 1084 (transgender-sports ban) — opposes LGBTQ promotion in school sports/policy (HARD).

0.note: DOCUMENTED CONTRARY (cross-party deviation): voted NO on SB 233 (Georgia Promise Scholarship vouchers), the flagship state school-choice bill, despite a campaign claim to 'increase choice in education' (HARD).

1.note: Voted YES on HB 1178 (Parents' Bill of Rights — curriculum access / parental authority) (HARD).

2.note: Voted YES on HB 1084, whose CRT-restriction component limits 'divisive concepts' instruction in K-12 (HARD).

1.note: Voted YES on SB 36 (Georgia Religious Freedom Restoration Act) — conscience/religious-liberty protection (HARD).

0.note: Voted YES on HB 1053 (prohibition on central bank digital currency) (HARD).

2.note: Voted NO on HB 221 (state-debt increase), scored as the fiscal-restraint position — opposes deficit/debt expansion (HARD).

1.note: Voted YES on HR 780 (citizen-only voting constitutional amendment) — citizenship verification (HARD).

0.note: Campaign: opposes defunding police, 'helped pass legislation that keeps law enforcement fully funded and increases funding for police training' (SOFT affirmative pro-law-enforcement).

1.note: Voted YES on SB 1 and SB 345 (prohibiting COVID vaccination-proof / vaccine-passport mandates) — declined federal mandate pressure (HARD).

4.note: Voted YES on SB 420 (restrictions on foreign-owned farmland) (HARD).

0.note: Voted YES on SB 319 (constitutional/permitless carry) (HARD).

profile.religion: Christian background (former Fellowship of Christian Athletes chapter president, Tuskegee ~1970; no current church affiliation documented)
profile.background: State senator SD-26 (Macon/Bibb + Hancock, Twiggs, Washington, Wilkinson cos., central GA) since Jan 2013; previously GA House from Jan 1975 — 2025 marked his 50th year in the General Assembly. B.S. Tuskegee; M.D. Mercer School of Medicine. Founding member of 100 Black Men. Committees: Appropriations (Ex-Officio), Health & Human Services, Insurance & Labor, Regulated Industries & Utilities, Transportation, Urban Affairs (Sec.). Confirmed running 2026 Democratic primary; won 2024 unopposed.

david-lucas@GA.notes_set: Long-serving progressive Democrat; uniformly liberal voting record with documented contrary positions on life, sexuality, school choice, voter ID, cash bail, immigration enforcement, and guns. No verified conservative cross-party deviation. HARD/verified: voted AGAINST HB 481 (heartbeat) and SPONSORED SR 136 (reproductive-freedom amendment) + SB 15 (remove 'unborn child' from natural-person definition) — 'Pro-Choice Champion'; voted NO on SB 185 (inmate gender-care ban, official roll #128, listed #8 in NO column); voted AGAINST SB 233 vouchers; OPPOSED SB 202 voter-ID (noted floor speech); voted NO on HB 1105 (anti-sanctuary, official roll #719 — NOT in the 34 YES); voted AGAINST SB 319 permitless carry (party-line, all Dems opposed) and sponsored gun-control (assault-weapon waiting period, SB 53 storage). METHOD NOTE: Freedom Index AND Grokipedia FALSELY listed conservative votes for Lucas (carry YES, HB 1105 YES, SB 185 YEA) that official GA Senate roll calls disprove — those aggregator directions were discarded; only verified contrary votes scored. Credit cells (police/federalism/economic specifics) null where no aligned evidence.

0.note: DOCUMENTED CONTRARY: sponsored SB 15 (2023) to remove 'unborn child' from Georgia's definition of 'natural person' and SR 136 (2023) constitutional amendment for 'reproductive freedom' — rejects personhood from conception (HARD).

1.note: DOCUMENTED CONTRARY: voted AGAINST HB 481 (2019 LIFE Act / heartbeat); labeled a 'Pro-Choice Champion' (HARD).

2.note: DOCUMENTED CONTRARY: voted NO on SB 185 (ban on state-funded gender-affirming care for prison inmates), official Senate roll #128 (listed #8 in the NO column), 3/3/2025 — against the transgender-restriction measure (HARD; refutes aggregator claims of a YES).

0.note: DOCUMENTED CONTRARY: voted AGAINST SB 233 (Georgia Promise Scholarship vouchers), Senate final 33-21 party-line (all 21 NO were Democrats), 3/20/2024 (HARD).

1.note: DOCUMENTED CONTRARY: openly opposed SB 202 (2021 Election Integrity Act / absentee voter-ID), delivering a noted floor speech calling its ID provisions voter suppression (HARD).

DOCUMENTED CONTRARY: SB 63 (cash-bail expansion) passed party-line with all Senate Democrats opposed (Feb 2024); Lucas voted with the Democratic minority against the bail-tightening measure (party-line marquee vote).

2.note: DOCUMENTED CONTRARY: voted NO on HB 1105 (anti-sanctuary / Criminal Alien Track and Report Act), official Senate roll #719 (NOT among the 34 YES votes), 3/21/2024 (HARD; refutes aggregator claim of a YES).

0.note: DOCUMENTED CONTRARY: SB 319 (permitless/constitutional carry) passed Senate party-line with all Democrats opposed, 3/1/2022; Lucas voted NO (party-line marquee vote; refutes aggregator claim of a YES).

1.note: DOCUMENTED CONTRARY: sponsored gun-control measures — a 10-day waiting period for assault-style weapons with enhanced background checks (2024) and SB 53 (firearm-storage education) — supports gun restrictions the rubric scores as contrary (HARD sponsorship).
